subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: The topic and purpose are communicated accurately and effectively; the focus on cultural identity through literary techniques remains clear and appropriate throughout.
The research question is clearly stated, narrowly focused, and consistently connected to each section of the discussion.
Section headings and the overall structure reinforce the central focus, ensuring seamless alignment with the research question.
weaknesses: The methodology description still lacks explicit detail on source-selection criteria and evaluation methods, limiting insight into the research process.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic communication and research question) fully meet the 5â6 band descriptors with clear, effective purpose and focus. Although the methodology strand is in the 3â4 band, the overall cohesion and clarity of purpose justify the top mark for this criterion.

strengths: Relevant literary terminology and concepts (imagery, metaphor, allusion) are applied correctly in most analyses.
Subject knowledge of the novelâs plot, characters, and historical context is clear and coherent.
Quotations and secondary sources are generally well integrated and support key arguments about cultural identity.
weaknesses: Terminology deployment is occasionally imprecise, and key concepts like cultural identity are handled in a somewhat general manner.
Some secondary sources are cited only in passing, with limited critical engagement or comparison of differing scholarly viewpoints.
Analytical depth varies, with sections reverting to description rather than deep conceptual exploration.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall performance sits solidly in the 3â4 band across application of sources, subject knowledge, and use of terminology. The student demonstrates clear understanding and appropriate use of evidence but does not consistently reach the sophisticated, deeply analytical level required for the top band.

strengths: A reasonable variety of primary and secondary sources are employed, and primary textual evidence is well chosen and pertinent.
Analysis moves beyond summary in many places, linking specific techniques back to the research question.
The evaluation acknowledges limitations and proposes avenues for future research, demonstrating reflective insight.
weaknesses: Evaluation and discussion are uneven; counter-arguments and alternative interpretations are not fully explored.
Several sections remain descriptive rather than analytical, and key literary devices (e.g., allusion, flashback) are not always examined as mechanisms of meaning.
Critical comparison between sources is limited, reducing the uniform relevance of research materials.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research quality and analysis depth align with the 4â6 band, while discussion and evaluation sit in the 1â3 band. Holistically, the work exhibits adequate to good critical thinking, meriting a score in the lower half of the 7â9 band.

strengths: The prescribed structure (title page, contents, numbered sections, conclusion, bibliography) is clearly and completely followed.
Layout elements (page numbering, consistent formatting, clear headings) are correctly applied and enhance readability.
Figures, italics, and boldface are used appropriately without distracting from the text.
weaknesses: Indentation and sub-section formatting in the contents page are slightly inconsistent, which can impede quick navigation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both structure and layout fully meet the descriptors for the top band. Minor inconsistencies in contents-page formatting do not detract from the overall clarity and completeness of the presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The research question is presented verbatim on the title page and again in the introduction: âHow does Ruta Sepetys employ literary techniquesâ¦?â  It is narrowly framed around one novel, one set of techniques and one thematic angle, demonstrating a clear and purposeful focus.

All subsequent sections (artwork, language, symbolism, etc.) map directly onto the wording of the question, showing an explicit connection between question and discussion.  Although some paragraphs lapse into description, the question itself remains precise and well centred, consistent with the highest band for this strand.

The analysis moves beyond summary by examining how specific techniques (e.g., Linaâs artwork as symbolism, language choices as markers of identity) illustrate cultural identity.  Several quotations are interpreted and linked back to the research focus, indicating genuine analytical effort.

Yet the depth remains uneven: some sections drift into extended narrative recount or sociological background without integrating these points into a sustained argument, and conclusions are sometimes asserted rather than rigorously built from evidence.  These features suggest an "adequate but partially relevant" level of analysisâappropriate to the lowerâmid part of the 4â6 band.

Quotations from the novel are well selected and usually integrated into discussion, and a range of secondary sources (journal articles, websites, psychological studies) are cited to support points about culture and identity.  This indicates generally relevant and appropriate application.

Yet, some secondary sources are only referenced in passing or used for definition rather than analytical development, and there is little critical engagement with differing viewpoints.  The application is therefore mostly relevant but not consistently so, fitting the 3â4 band.

Essential presentation elementsâtitle page, clear word count, page numbers, and a functional table of contentsâare all present.  Citations follow a consistent style and the bibliography is alphabetised.  Illustrations, italics and boldface are used appropriately without impeding readability.

Spacing, font size and margin conventions appear to meet IB requirements, and there are no significant layout errors.  Consequently, the work satisfies the descriptor for a fully correct layout, warranting the top score in the 3â4 band.

The student demonstrates clear understanding of plot, character and historical setting, and can explain how techniques such as symbolism, imagery and flashback convey cultural identity.  References to concepts like "juxtaposition" and "metaphor" show a sound if sometimes surface-level grasp of literary analysis.

Nonetheless, parts of the discussion become descriptive recounting rather than analytical exploration, and occasional generalisations suggest that knowledge, while clear, is not always deeply developed.  This aligns with the 3â4 band for subject knowledge.

The report follows the prescribed EE structure: title page, abstract omitted as per new guide, contents page, numbered sections, conclusion and bibliography.  Headings and sub-headings create a clear line of argument and assist navigation, fulfilling the requirement for an appropriate and complete structure.

While coherent, some sections (e.g., multiple "2." headings) could be better balanced and more analytical signposting would aid the reader.  These minor issues place the work at the lower end of the top band (3â4).

The essay draws on a reasonable variety of secondary sources (academic articles, cultural studies texts, author website, etc.) which are generally pertinent to the research question.  Primary textual evidence is plentiful and well chosen.  Citations are mostly accurate and a full bibliography is supplied, demonstrating adequate research practice.

Some sources are popular or pedagogical rather than scholarly, and there is limited critical comparison between sources, so the relevance is not uniformly strong.  Overall, the research is solid and clearly linked to the question, meeting the expectations of the 4â6 band, mid-range.

The student states the topic immediately on the title page and restates it in the introduction, then links it explicitly to the purpose of analysing cultural identity through literary techniques.  Throughout the essay the chosen focus (cultural identity in Between Shades of Gray) is kept in view and section headings are consistently tied back to that focus.

Minor lapses appear â for example, the sub-sections sometimes drift into plot summary and definitions of culture rather than sustained textual analysis â yet the central purpose remains easy to follow.  Because the communication is clear, sustained and audience-appropriate, the work fulfils the descriptor for the 5â6 band, albeit nearer the lower end due to occasional loss of sharpness.

The conclusion restates main findings and briefly acknowledges limitations, showing an attempt at evaluation.  There is some synthesis of how different literary techniques collectively convey cultural identity, and the student recognises areas for future research.

However, the discussion of implications is brief and tends to affirm rather than critically interrogate claims; counter-arguments or alternative interpretations are not explored.  The reasoning occasionally becomes repetitive and descriptive, leading to gaps in coherence.  The argument therefore sits in the 1â3 band as "adequate but containing noticeable inconsistencies," meriting a 3.

The student outlines using Sepetysâs novel as the primary source and consulting secondary literature on Lithuanian culture and identity.  A formal bibliography and in-text referencing are provided, indicating conscious source selection.

However, the essay offers only a brief, rather general description of research methods.  There is limited justification of why particular critical or historical sources were chosen, and no explanation of how evidence was located or evaluated.  The methodology is therefore mostly complete and shows some informed choices but lacks the clarity and rigour expected for the top band, placing it securely in the 3â4 range.

Literary terms such as âimagery,â âallusion,â âjuxtaposition,â and âmetaphorâ are used correctly in several instances, indicating familiarity with essential concepts.  The student also references ideas like âfirst-person narrationâ and âsymbolic acts,â showing an attempt to apply critical vocabulary.

However, the deployment of terms is occasionally imprecise (e.g., labelling basic description as âjuxtapositionâ) and key concepts such as âcultural identityâ are sometimes handled in a general rather than discipline-specific manner.  This places the work at the lower end of the mostly-accurate 3â4 band.

annotations.comment: The student has included a precise word count within the prescribed limit, reflecting attention to formal IB requirements.
The table of contents is comprehensive and accurately maps section headings to page numbers, facilitating easy navigation of the essay.
The discussion of Kucios would be strengthened by analysing specific literary techniquesâsuch as juxtaposition and toneârather than describing the tradition alone.
The research question and topic are prominently and clearly stated on the title page, demonstrating excellent alignment with the essayâs focus and ensuring immediate clarity of purpose.
The opening paragraph of the introduction is heavily narrative and plot-driven, which delays engagement with the research focus and limits immediate analytical depth.
The methodology section lacks specific detail on source selection criteria and evaluation methods; the student should elaborate on search strategies and justify chosen materials.
The analysis of dialogue would be more rigorous if it examined rhetorical featuresâsuch as syntax, repetition, and vocabulary choiceâto show how solidarity is constructed.
The bibliography demonstrates a wide range of primary and secondary sources, reflecting thorough engagement with relevant scholarship.
Contextualizing the Kucios celebration enriches cultural understanding and demonstrates relevance of tradition as an identity marker.
Indentation and formatting of sub-sections in the contents page are inconsistent, which can disrupt the readerâs ability to quickly distinguish main sections from sub-sections.
The analysis of the allusion to Munchâs The Scream describes its emotional effect but omits examination of how allusion operates as a literary device to reinforce cultural memory.
The section header clearly signals the start of the literature analysis chapter, reinforcing structure and reader orientation.
The student provides a clear, sourced definition of âcultural identity,â establishing a solid conceptual framework for subsequent analysis.
Citation of âSepetys et al. (2021)â lacks full bibliographic details and critical engagement; the student should integrate explicit art-historical theory when discussing Munch.
The section on language and identity would benefit from engagement with code-switching theory or sociolinguistic frameworks to deepen the argument.
Discussion of Linaâs artwork relies on narrative summary; deeper exploration of how imagery and metaphor function to construct cultural identity would strengthen analysis.
Selection of quotations about the protagonistâs drawings effectively supports the theme of art as cultural resistance.
Insightful recognition of first-person narration as a lens for conveying internal conflict between cultural heritage and oppressive language pressures.
Use of dialogue to illustrate community bonds under duress effectively demonstrates the social dimension of cultural identity.
The bibliography formatting is inconsistent (italics usage, element order, missing access dates); apply a single citation style for coherence.
The interpretation of singing the national anthem as a symbolic act of resistance is well supported and highlights cultural defiance effectively.
The limitations paragraph thoughtfully acknowledges scope constraints and proposes avenues for future research, strengthening evaluation.
This narrative-heavy account of the forced signing scene could be condensed to maintain analytical momentum and sharpen focus on the cultural implications.
Incorporation of Savickaâs theoretical perspective on identity adds valuable interdisciplinary depth to the analysis of memory as a lifeline.
Flashback discussion remains descriptive; further analysis of narrative time shifts and their thematic impact would enhance critical depth.
The conclusion summarizes findings but lacks synthesis of broader implications and critical reflection on how the research question was resolved.
